behavior_surfaces: large workflow template tool-call payload streaming
state_contracts: Tools may opt into eagerInputStreaming when they commonly receive large JSON inputs.
toolToAPISchema forwards eager_input_streaming only to first-party Anthropic API endpoints that accept the beta field.
workflow_template_authoring opts into eager input streaming so large template payloads are streamed instead of relying on global fine-grained tool streaming flags.
MCP list-tools publication must prefer a tool's explicit inputJSONSchema over Zod conversion when the explicit schema exists.
MCP list-tools publication returns a cloned schema so external consumers cannot mutate the tool-owned schema object.
known_unknowns: This does not remove provider hard request-size limits; it makes this large-input tool use the existing first-party eager input streaming path where supported.
Third-party Anthropic-compatible proxies, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, and Azure OpenAI still do not receive eager_input_streaming because those paths can reject the beta field.
The running desktop sidecar may need rebuild/restart before source edits are reflected, depending on how it was launched.
confidence_notes: Focused tests prove the tool advertises eager input streaming, accepts a template payload larger than 100000 serialized characters for validation, and keeps the API-bound schema object-root contract.
MCP coverage proves workflow_template_authoring publishes its explicit template object schema instead of the discriminated-union oneOf Zod fallback.
Full server-area check passed after the focused tests.
user_decisions: User clarified the issue is the workflow_template_authoring template tool-call input size, not workflow phase policy or workflows.json persistence size.
User asked to make that input allowance larger.

boundary.scope: Increase workflow_template_authoring large template input handling through tool schema/streaming paths.
excluded: Globally enabling eager input streaming for all tools
Forwarding unsupported beta fields to non-first-party providers
